reasoning: At 1x the key silhouette collapses into a thin indistinct stick — the head detail is completely lost and the bow reads as a smudge, not a recognizable friendly shape. Sticker test fails at 1x. At 4x the pixel art is competent: the checkered bow has personality and the teal/orange accent palette adds warmth. On dark bg the grey shaft has poor separation from background. On light bg it reads better but the bow still feels busy rather than charming — lots of small pixel details that add visual noise instead of a clean round-soft silhouette. The key has mild charm (cute bow top, warm palette) but the silhouette clarity is below threshold for instant readability. Charm and clarity are both mid — neither strength compensates the other's weakness. Marginal pass leaning toward fail on silhouette criterion.
